pi_items.source: Verified from setup_opentoonz.sh and setup_task.sh extracting official OpenToonz samples.
Hardcoded and verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh.
Specified in task description and checked in verifier.py. The source scene has 73 frames, making this possible.

task.json metadata claims 50. The verifier uses MAX_BRIGHTNESS = 80. The script comments claim the original scene has a 'white background' but source files include sky/trees (mean brightness 100-190). True inverted brightness cannot be determined accurately without a native OpenToonz render.
verifier.py checks std_dev > MIN_STD where MIN_STD = 10 to ensure the image is not a solid color.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires applying a negative (invert) filter to the 'dwanko_run.tnz' sample animation and rendering at least 12 PNG frames to an output directory. The source scene is located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz' and comes from the official OpenToonz samples repository. The rendered output must go to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/negative_fx/'. The verification script ensures that the generated images have a standard deviation greater than 10 to avoid solid color outputs.
